storage.description: Currently rollup config runner only supports storage path parameter

oneOf.description: Blocks are produced at fixed time intervals, regardless of whether there are transactions. This means empty blocks may be created.

block_time_ms.description: The interval, in milliseconds, at which new blocks are produced.

oneOf.description: A new block is produced only when a batch blob (but not a proof blob) is submitted. Each block contains exactly one batch blob and zero or more proof blobs.

block_wait_timeout_ms.description: The maximum time [`sov_rollup_interface::node::da::DaService::get_block_at`] will wait for a block to become available. If this timeout elapses, an error is returned. If set to `None`, [`DEFAULT_BLOCK_WAITING_TIME_MS`] is used.

oneOf.description: A new block is produced when either a batch blob or a proof blob is submitted. Each block contains exactly one blob.

block_wait_timeout_ms.description: The maximum time [`sov_rollup_interface::node::da::DaService::get_block_at`] will wait for a block to become available. If this timeout elapses, an error is returned. If set to `None`, [`DEFAULT_BLOCK_WAITING_TIME_MS`] is used.

oneOf.description: Blocks are created manually, with no automatic production.

CorsConfiguration.description:
See [`HttpServerConfig::cors`].

# Default

CORS makes local development easier, so it's enabled by default.

In production, one may want to disable it and let your reverse proxy handle CORS instead.

oneOf.description: Enables CORS with a permissive policy, intended for local development.

HexHash.description: 32 bytes in hexadecimal format, with `0x` prefix.

HttpServerConfig.description: Configuration for HTTP server(s) exposed by the node.

cors.description: Enable or disable CORS policy headers. Enabled by default.

public_address.description: The fully qualified public name of the server, in case the rollup node is running behind a proxy. For instance: ```toml public_address = "https://rollup.example.com" ```

MockAddress.description: Mock address; 32 bytes in hex-encoded format

connection_string.description: Connection string to the database for storing Da Data. - "sqlite://demo_data/da.sqlite?mode=rwc" - "sqlite::memory:" - "postgresql://root:hunter2@aws.amazon.com/mock-da"

finalization_blocks.description: Defines how many blocks progress to finalization.

randomization.description: If specified, [`StorableMockDaLayer`] will add randomization to non-finalized blocks.

sender_address.description: The address to use to "submit" blobs on the mock da layer.

max_datagram_size.description:
Defines how many measurements a rollup node will accumulate before sending it to the Telegraf. It is expected from the rollup node to produce metrics all the time, so measurements are buffered by size and not sent by time. and below 67 KB, which is the maximal UDP packet size. It also means that if a single serialized metric is larger than this value, a UDP packet will be larger.

Note: to disable buffering, set this value to `Some(1)`.

max_pending_metrics.description: How many metrics are allowed to be in pending state, before new metrics will be dropped. This is a number of metrics, not serialized bytes. The total number of bytes to be held in memory might vary per metric + `max_datagram_size`

telegraf_address.description: UDP socket where Telegraf service is active, something like 127.0.0.1:8094. Localhost is preferred for latency and reliability reasons.

minimum_profit_per_tx.description: The minimum fee that the preferred sequencer is willing to accept, denominated in rollup tokens. Defaults to zero. Sequencers should set this to a non-zero value if they wish to cover their DA costs.

aggregated_proof_block_jump.description: The "distance" measured in the number of blocks between two consecutive aggregated proofs.

max_number_of_transitions_in_db.description: A number of state transition info entries are allowed to be stored in the database. When the number is exceeded, older entries are removed.

max_number_of_transitions_in_memory.description: A number of state transition info entries are allowed to be kept in memory. If the number is exceeded, rollup execution will be blocked until provers cathes up.

prover_address.description: The prover receives rewards to this address.

oneOf.description: Blobs inside a single blob are going to be out of order, but blobs will never pass the block boundary.
Blobs in non-finalized blocks are going to be shuffled across all non-finalized blobs. The height of the chain is not going to be changed.

drop_percent.description: The percentage of blobs is going to be skipped forever.

oneOf.description: The height of the chain is going to be rewound to some height between last finalized and current.

RandomizationConfig.description: Configuration of randomization for non-finalized blocks.

concurrent_sync_tasks.description: How many concurrent tasks to get block from DA service

da_polling_interval_ms.description: Polling interval for the DA service to check the sync status (in milliseconds).

oneOf.description: A standard batch builder, which can post transactions to the rollup but not give soft confirmations.
A "Preferred" batch builder which is allowed to give soft confirmations.

admin_addresses.description: The list of addresses that are allowed to perform admin operations on the sequencer.

automatic_batch_production.description: When enabled, submitted transactions are periodically assembled into batches and automatically posted to the DA layer. When disabled, the batch production endpoint has to be called explicitly.

dropped_tx_ttl_secs.description:
For how many seconds the sequencer keeps track of dropped transactions after being done with them.

Larger values result in higher memory usage, but better tx status tracking for users.

max_allowed_blocks_behind.description: The sequencer won't process incoming requests unless the node is within this many blocks behind the DA chain head.

max_batch_size_bytes.description: Maximum size of a batch. The batch builder will not build batches larger than this size.

mempool_max_txs_count.description: Maximum number of transactions in mempool. Once this limit is reached, the batch builder will evict older transactions.

path.description: Path that can be utilized by concrete implementation
